---
title: "MSE workflow"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.html >}}

<div class="paper-title">From Assessment Conditioning to CMP Evidence</div>

This page condenses SCW17/Paper-04. It locates the documentation site within
the broader analytical system without duplicating that system.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  J["JJM assessment configurations"] --> C["Condition operating models"]
  C --> O["OM / OEM / IEM bundles"]
  O --> M["Apply CMPs in jmMSE"]
  M --> P["Performance metrics and diagnostics"]
  P --> D["CMP comparison and decision"]
  D --> S["Versioned specification in jmMSE26"]
  S --> Y["Annual operational application"]
  Y --> V["Archived inputs, calculations, and advice"]
```

| Layer | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| JJM | Assessment-conditioned stock and fishery dynamics |
| `jjmR` / `FLjjm` | Reading JJM output and constructing FLR/MSE objects |
| FLR / `mse` / `msemodules` | Projection objects and generic MP machinery |
| `jmMSE` | OM definitions, CMP code, tuning, simulation, and performance |
| `jmMSE26` | Reviewed specifications, selection evidence, and application instructions |

## Separation of responsibilities

`jmMSE26` must not become an independent fork of the MSE engine. Executable
snippets here are conformance examples or thin wrappers pinned to a `jmMSE`
commit. Operating models, recruitment assumptions, movement assumptions,
reference points, and performance metrics remain governed by their scientific
source repositories.

## Reproducibility chain

Every performance claim should identify the OM set, CMP version, random-seed
policy, iteration count, projection period, metric definition, output artifact,
and source commit. Every operational application should identify its input data
vintage and the adopted CMP version.
